name: Atelier Modules
label: Atelier Modules
description: Four apps that set up a sharper Claude Code — Session Codes + the status bar, the Horse Browser night console, CLAUDE.md, and gwx — plus catalyst-chrome, the theme they render in.

apps.name: Status Bar
apps.tagline: A name for every agent, and the terminal line that wears it.
apps.description: **Session Codes + the Claude Code status bar.** Every session earns a tiny identity — callsign, colour, emoji — from one hash the terminal, the dashboard, and the browser all agree on. Part 01 teaches the identity engine with a live rolling decoder and the 48-codename grid; part 02 dissects the statusline piece by piece, then wires it into `~/.claude/settings.json` with one click (merged, never clobbered — backed up first). The page honestly reports what's wired: this module's script, the same codename script from another copy, a different statusline, or nothing.

apps.name: Horse Browser
apps.tagline: Give your agents a browser of their own — logged in, and never in your way.
apps.description: **The night console.** A second browser just for agents — signed in once, quietly shared, never stealing your screen. Built on browser-harness (~600 lines of raw-CDP engine vs ~120k for Playwright — the bitter lesson of agent harnesses) and the dedicated Horse Browser, installed and updated from npm (`@pa1nd/horse-browser`). The page shows a live wall of your real stack: agent sessions, the harness daemons driving them, and the actual tabs they have open — name-matched by session codename.

apps.name: CLAUDE.md
apps.tagline: A note Claude reads before it writes a line.
apps.description: **The note Claude reads first.** The four rules that went viral — Think Before Coding, Simplicity First, Surgical Changes, Goal-Driven Execution — with the story of where they came from, and a modal that renders the full skill. Your real `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` is read live, chapter by chapter, with the four-rules chapter lit when present; one click appends the block (never clobbers, always backed up). Also tracks the Horse Browser playbooks import that shares the same file.

apps.name: gwx
apps.tagline: Your whole Google Workspace, in your agent's hands.
apps.description: **One multi-account command over your whole Google Workspace.** Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs, Sheets and more — reads fan out across every account in parallel, and a library of 96 ready-made agent skills (per-service helpers, multi-step recipes, whole personas) loads on demand, browsable in a searchable catalogue. A skill + a CLI beats an MCP: the agent gets the whole command surface, not a hand-picked menu. Installed and updated from npm (`@pa1nd/gwx`), with live per-account sign-in state.

apps.name: Catalyst Chrome
apps.tagline: The Catalyst-based theme these modules render in.
apps.description: The **chrome** the collection's modules pin (`meta.chrome = 'catalyst-chrome'`) — a Tailwind Catalyst-based theme that owns the rail, topbar, tokens, and dark mode, and publishes the `@atelier/kit` primitives (Button, Dialog, Table, …) companion modules import.
